channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Cointelegraph - Security, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading, Blockthreat, CryptoPotato, CryptoSlate, Krebs On Security, The Hacker News, Coindesk, GroupIB Blog, Data Breach Today, CNN.com - Crypto News, DataBreaches.net

item.title: Did you sign up for the new White House app? Don’t use it until you read this!
item.description: Did you sign up for the new White House app? Don’t use it until you read this, because it puts your privacy and data security at risk. Patrick Quirk takes an impressive technical piece and distills it...

item.title: CareCloud notifies the SEC after attack on one of its EHR environments
item.description: DysruptionHub reports: CareCloud said an unauthorized third party briefly disrupted one of its six electronic health record environments on March 16, restoring access that evening as investigators ass...

item.title: Institutions are paying Bitcoin custodians for the privilege of added risk
item.description: Institutions pay custodians for illusory safety. Bitcoin's onchain governance eliminates counterparty risk that traditional models reintroduce....
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph - Security

item.title: Thankfully, the Infinite Campus incident did not involve a lot of non-directory student information
item.description: In the wake of the Infinite Campus data breach, DataBreaches was contacted by several concerned EdTech professionals who weren’t prepared to accept Infinite Campus’s word that there was no sensitive s...

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Breach FBI Director’s Personal Email, Hit Stryker With Wiper Attack
item.description: Threat actors with ties to Iran successfully broke into the personal email account of Kash Patel, the director of the U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), and leaked a cache of photos and other...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Iran-linked group Handala hacked FBI Director Kash Patel’s personal email account
item.description: Pierluigi Paganini reports: Iran-linked hacking group Handala claims it breached FBI Director Kash Patel’s personal Gmail account and shared alleged data, including photos and files. The FBI confirmed...

item.title: Woodfords Family Services notifying patients and families about 2024 ransomware attack
item.description: A notice by Woodfords Family Services in Maine caught my eye because the name sounded familiar. They provide support services for people with disabilities and their families. On March 27, 2026, they i...

item.title: Thousands of Corewell Health patients affected by security breach
item.description: Fox2 reports: Corewell Health announced their former health care consulting vendor experienced a data breach in 2024, affecting thousands of patients. Pinnacle Holdings previously provided health care...

item.title: Meet Claude Mythos: Leaked Anthropic post reveals the powerful upcoming model
item.description: Matt Binder reports: An accidental leak has now been officially confirmed by AI company Anthropic regarding its most powerful AI model yet. The model, now known as “Claude Mythos,” was originally unco...
item.dc:creator: DataBreaches.net

item.title: Citrix NetScaler Under Active Recon for CVE-2026-3055 (CVSS 9.3) Memory Overread Bug
item.description: A recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway is witnessing active reconnaissance activity, according to Defused Cyber and watchTowr. The vulnerabili...

item.title: CISA Adds CVE-2025-53521 to KEV After Active F5 BIG-IP APM Exploitation
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Friday added a critical security flaw impacting F5 BIG-IP Access Policy Manager (APM) to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) c...
